Position early and let the decade do the work. AI Infrastructure. The backbone everything runs on. The data centers, the compute, the owned infrastructure powering every model on earth. Every other sector on this list runs on top of it. Space. Connectivity and infrastructure moving off the planet and into orbit. Direct to phone networks, launch, and satellite constellations turning space into the next commercial frontier. Photonics. The plumbing that moves the data. As clusters scale to hundreds of thousands of GPUs, moving information at the speed of light becomes the constraint. A market tripling this decade as 800G goes to 1.6T goes to 3.2T. Nuclear Energy. The only power source that can actually feed AI at scale. Data centers need enormous, constant, carbon free baseload, and the grid cannot deliver it fast enough. Nuclear is the answer the entire industry is racing toward. Memory. The bottleneck nobody can build fast enough. Every AI server needs 8 to 10 times the memory of a normal one, the makers are sold out, and the shortage runs for years. No memory, no AI. Humanoid Robotics. AI stepping out of the screen and into the physical world. A market going from under $2 billion today to $38 billion by 2035. The companies making the brains, eyes, and sensors win no matter which robot wins. Quantum Computing. The frontier that breaks the limits of classical computing entirely. Still early, still small, but governments and the biggest tech companies on earth are pouring billions in. The most asymmetric bet of the group. Seven sectors. One direction. text:
CPO 연재 총정리 — 빛이 칩 안으로 들어오는 순간

6편에 걸친 CPO(Co-Packaged Optics) 연재의 요약본. 기술 핵심은 짧게, 투자 함의는 무게감 있게

핵심 메시지

1. CPO는 "되느냐 안 되느냐"의 기술이 아니라 "언제, 어디서부터 되느냐"의 기술이다.

2. AI 추론은 연산보다 데이터 이동에 전력을 더 많이 쓴다. ASIC 전력의 최대 절반이 칩 간 데이터 이동에 들어간다.

3. 구리는 전력벽(power wall)에 도달했다. 짧은 거리에서는 광학이 답이지만, 광학 부품도 전자 회로만큼 작아져야 그 이득을 실제로 누릴 수 있다.

4. Nvidia 로드맵은 2단계다. 2026년 Rubin(Spectrum6 스위치)에서 스케일아웃에 먼저 CPO 적용, 2028년 Feynman(NVLink8)에서 스케일업까지 확장.

5.같은 데이터를 보고도 Nvidia·Meta는 "이제 시작"이라 하고, Google·Broadcom은 "아직 아니다"라고 한다. 벤더 락인 우려와 2018년 온보드 옵틱스(OBO) 실패 경험이 후자의 신중론을 뒷받침한다.

기술 검증: 3가지 Kill Condition

6. 파이버 정렬 — 광파이버(8~10μm)와 실리콘 도파관(500nm급)을 50nm 오차 안에서 붙여야 한다. 일반 전기 정렬보다 100배 정밀해야 한다. 해법은 여러 개 동시에 검증되는 단계까지 왔지만, 표준이 하나로 수렴하지 않았다.

7. 레이저 신뢰성 — 생산된 레이저의 최대 50%가 스펙 미달이다. 업계는 ELSFP(레이저를 패키지 밖으로 분리)로 리스크를 줄이고, 중복화(Lightmatter)·다중화(Ranovus 퀀텀닷) 두 방향으로 신뢰성을 끌어올리는 중이다.

8. 테스트 데이터 통합 — 전기(STDF)와 광학(CSV/XLS) 검사 데이터가 서로 다른 포맷으로 따로 쌓인다. 전기 99%×광학 99% = 98% 수율로, 곱셈 구조가 양산 규모에서 손실을 키운다. 업계 중론은 표준화에 최소 2~3년 추가 소요.

9. 세 조건 중 가장 화려하지 않은 3번(테스트 데이터)이 CPO 전체 일정을 가장 오래 잡아끌 가능성이 높다.

투자 관점 — 누가 돈을 버는가

10. 파운드리 — $TSM: 패키지 안 모든 칩렛이 TSMC PDK·공정을 따라야 하는 구조적 락인. Nvidia·AMD·Google·AWS 가속기가 TSMC 패키징을 쓰는 한 광학 엔진 칩렛도 자동으로 TSMC산이 될 가능성. 다만 현재 실리콘 포토닉스 파운드리 시장은 연 10억 달러 미만으로 TSMC 매출의 1%도 안 됨 — 8배 성장 전망이 있지만 출발선 자체가 작다는 점은 균형 있게 봐야 함.

11. 레이저 — $COHR, $LITE: Coherent·Lumentum·Sumitomo 3사가 글로벌 레이저 시장 68% 점유. 레이저 시장은 2024년 200억 달러 돌파, 2030년 300억 달러 이상 전망. $NVDA가 두 회사에 각각 20억 달러 투자해 캐파 선점 — 공급이 수요를 못 따라가는 구간에서 자본이 먼저 줄을 선 신호. Lumentum 자체 추정 InP 광 레인 수요 CAGR 약 85%, 캐파 증설 속도가 계속 못 따라잡는 격차 존재.

12. OCS(광 회로 스위치) — 신흥 전장: CignalAI 추정 TAM 2028~2029년 연 30억 달러 이상. Google 외주화로 Lumentum·Coherent의 매출 기회가 몇 분기 만에 1억→4억 달러로 점프, 단일 고객 10억 달러 계약도 등장. Lumentum R300(MEMS 기반)이 검증된 강자, iPronics 같은 웨이브가이드(SiP) 신생 기업이 정면 도전 — CPO 본류보다 승자가 더 빨리 가려질 수 있는 영역.

13. 시장 규모 종합: pluggable 광모듈 시장 2023년 60억→2030년 250억 달러(Coherent), 광 컴포넌트 시장 2030년 250억 달러(OMDIA)/2029년 310억 달러(CignalAI), 패키징 포함 실리콘 포토닉스 IC 시장 2031~2034년 290억~320억 달러(마진율 50%+ 전망).

14. 인프라 자본 집약도: Ayar Labs·Wiwynn 시연 랙 기준 100kW 이상 전력 밀도, 100% 액체 냉각, HVDC 전력 아키텍처 필요. CPO 도입은 칩 설계 문제를 넘어 랙 전체 전력·냉각 인프라 교체를 요구하는 자본 집약적 사업.

15. 지정학 변수: 중국은 150mm 리튬나이오베이트 웨이퍼 자체 개발(CHIPX), 일본 257억 달러 투입, 인도 포토닉스 팹 최대 50% 자본 지원, 유럽은 M&A로 규모 확대 중. 인력 부족(2025년 상반기 R&D 공석 330개+)과 CEO 교체(IPG Photonics, Lumentum)는 업계 리더십 불안정성의 신호.

$TSM $COHR $LITE $NVDA 

매수, 매도 등 투자권유 아님.

Lumentum R300(MEMS 기반)이 검증된 강자, iPronics 같은 웨이브가이드(SiP) 신생 기업이 정면 도전 — CPO 본류보다 승자가 더 빨리 가려질 수 있는 영역. 13. 시장 규모 종합: pluggable 광모듈 시장 2023년 60억→2030년 250억 달러(Coherent), 광 컴포넌트 시장 2030년 250억 달러(OMDIA)/2029년 310억 달러(CignalAI), 패키징 포함 실리콘 포토닉스 IC 시장 2031~2034년 290억~320억 달러(마진율 50%+ 전망). 14. 인프라 자본 집약도: Ayar Labs·Wiwynn 시연 랙 기준 100kW 이상 전력 밀도, 100% 액체 냉각, HVDC 전력 아키텍처 필요. CPO 도입은 칩 설계 문제를 넘어 랙 전체 전력·냉각 인프라 교체를 요구하는 자본 집약적 사업. 15. 지정학 변수: 중국은 150mm 리튬나이오베이트 웨이퍼 자체 개발(CHIPX), 일본 257억 달러 투입, 인도 포토닉스 팹 최대 50% 자본 지원, 유럽은 M&A로 규모 확대 중. 인력 부족(2025년 상반기 R&D 공석 330개+)과 CEO 교체(IPG Photonics, Lumentum)는 업계 리더십 불안정성의 신호. $TSM $COHR $LITE $NVDA 매수, 매도 등 투자권유 아님."}}}, "previous_counts": {"bookmark_count": 0, "favorite_count": 0, "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 0}, "rest_id": "2069927317850427532", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1103", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
It’s one thing to argue AI equities are in a bubble, but another to suggest the underlying buildout isn’t real.

The valuation argument may be correct, but it’s separate from the physical reality underneath.

Power, fabrication, photonics, and heat rejection remain scarce, capital-intensive, and difficult to scale.

Stocks can fall without changing what’s physically been built.

text:
Elon Musk’s acquisition of Mesh puts the entire optical-interconnect sector on the strategic map.

Mesh builds the optical systems.

Sivers $SIVE develops the high-performance lasers and photonics technology that can power them. https://t.co/n7EwBJh4WN

Here is what Putin spoke:
"I am pleased to welcome the participants and organizers of the 13th Forum of Regions of Russia and Belarus, which traditionally takes place on the eve of Independence Day of the Republic of Belarus, celebrated on July 3 and marking the anniversary of Minsk's liberation from the Nazi invaders.

I sincerely congratulate all Belarusian friends on this national holiday. Russia will always remember that during the Great Patriotic War, our peoples, shoulder to shoulder and at enormous sacrifice, defended freedom and independence, bringing peace to Europe and all of humanity.

It is important that Russia and Belarus carefully preserve and enhance their fraternal traditions of friendship and mutual assistance, strengthening their historically established cultural and spiritual ties. Russian-Belarusian relations are truly strategic, and our Union State serves as a model of mutually beneficial and equal integration, effectively facilitating the deepening of cooperation within such associations as the Eurasian Economic Union, the Commonwealth of Independent States, and the Collective Security Treaty Organization.

One of the main driving forces behind the partnership between Russia and Belarus, as well as the construction of the Union State, are ties between regions. This is the solid foundation upon which cooperation between the two countries is steadily developing across a wide range of areas—political and security, social and humanitarian, and, of course, economic.

Forums of Regions regularly bring together representatives of federal and local authorities, legislators and businesspeople, experts, and public figures—in short, all those who help establish and develop direct contacts between Russian and Belarusian regions.

This year, more than 600 delegates from 73 constituent entities of the Russian Federation arrived in the capital of Belarus. Among them are governors, heads of regional legislative assemblies, deputies, ministers, and company executives. The Belarusian delegation is no less prestigious.

The substantial package of agreements and commercial contracts signed during the forum demonstrates our mutual focus on achieving concrete results.

You are discussing a truly relevant topic: "Economic Interaction between Regions – the Foundation for Sustainable Development of Russia and Belarus." I note that, thanks in large part to close interregional economic cooperation, bilateral trade turnover last year broke another record – $52 billion, according to our statistics. This year, growth continues – more than 7 percent in the first quarter. All mutual settlements between our countries are conducted in national currencies.

The harmonization of legislation in trade, investment, and financial regulation is actively underway. Approaches in customs and tax matters are being unified, and a unified quality control system for goods is being implemented. The Union State Committee on Standardization and Quality held its first meeting during the forum.

Russia leads the way in accumulated investment in the Belarusian economy, at approximately $4 billion. Companies with Russian capital operate in all regions of the republic, with approximately 2,500 of them nationwide.

The regions are closely involved in the implementation of 26 joint import substitution projects in high-tech sectors. For example, modern mining equipment is being manufactured with Belarusian partners in the Moscow Region, machine tool components are being produced in the Novosibirsk Region, and equipment from the Minsk Tractor Plant is being assembled in Tatarstan.

Production cooperation has been established between Belarusian civil aviation plants and the interregional cluster of the Irkutsk, Ulyanovsk, Nizhny Novgorod, and Voronezh Regions, which produces the MS-21 and Sukhoi Superjet 100 airliners. Design bureaus in the Sverdlovsk and Brest Regions are working on the Osvey light multirole aircraft project.

Leading Belarusian factories Integral and Planar supply components for electronics manufacturing in Tatarstan and the Nizhny Novgorod and Moscow regions. Companies in Moscow, St. Petersburg, and Minsk have established effective cooperation chains for the production of optical devices and photonics instruments for communications, data transmission, and IT systems."

Regular passenger flights operate from 10 Russian regions to Belarus. Cross-border commuter trains between Smolensk and Vitebsk and between Smolensk and Orsha were launched in April. Service between Velikiye Luki and Vitebsk, Velikiye Luki and Polotsk, and Bryansk and Gomel are next in line. Plans include the construction of a high-speed railway between Moscow and Minsk.

The railway and port infrastructure of Russian regions is being used to transport Belarusian exports. A permit-free transit procedure is being gradually introduced for Russian and Belarusian goods transported to third countries by road.

Leading Belarusian factories Integral and Planar supply components for electronics manufacturing in Tatarstan and the Nizhny Novgorod and Moscow regions. Companies in Moscow, St. Petersburg, and Minsk have established effective cooperation chains for the production of optical devices and photonics instruments for communications, data transmission, and IT systems.

Regular passenger flights operate from 10 Russian regions to Belarus. Cross-border commuter trains between Smolensk and Vitebsk and between Smolensk and Orsha were launched in April. Service between Velikiye Luki and Vitebsk, Velikiye Luki and Polotsk, and Bryansk and Gomel are next in line. Plans include the construction of a high-speed railway between Moscow and Minsk.

The railway and port infrastructure of Russian regions is being used to transport Belarusian exports. A permit-free transit procedure is being gradually introduced for Russian and Belarusian goods transported to third countries by road."

text:
$SIVE   AI분석

1. $SIVE (Sivers Semiconductors) 핵심 사업과 최근 뉴스
Sivers Semiconductors는 스웨덴 회사로, 크게 두 축을 가지고 있습니다:
•  Photonics 부문: InP 기반 고출력·협대역 CW DFB 레이저와 레이저 어레이. AI 데이터센터 광연결(CPO), FMCW LiDAR에 특화.
•  Wireless 부문: mmWave 빔포머 IC (5G, Satcom, defense).
2026년 3월 발표된 LiDAR 뉴스가 정확히 사용자가 말한 내용입니다:
•  전략적 LiDAR 고객이 자사 기술을 플랫폼 전체에 채택.
•  2026년 Q4부터 생산 ramp 시작 (자동차 + 산업/로보틱스용).
•  공급 품목: CW DFB 레이저 + 광증폭기.
•  누적 수익 잠재력 $53M ~ $138M (고객 제품 수명 주기 기준).
이 고객은 공식 확인은 안 됐지만, 시장에서 Aeva로 강력하게 추정되고 있습니다. 이유:
•  Aeva는 FMCW LiDAR 전문 (협대역·고출력 레이저가 핵심).
•  Aeva + LG Innotek 전략 파트너십 (2025년 7월 발표, LG Innotek이 Aeva 모듈 제조·공급).
•  Aeva는 로보틱스·휴머노이드·산업 자동화도 타겟팅하고 있음 (Boston Dynamics Atlas 같은 고급 비전 시스템에 잘 맞는 기술).
이게 바로 사용자가 말한 “$AEVA와 LG Innotek을 통해 공급망으로 들어온다”는 부분입니다. 2026년 말 생산 확대 + 상당한 수익 잠재력도 정확히 맞아요.
2. $SPCX (SpaceX 프록시) Mesh Optical 인수와의 연결고리
최근 뉴스 (2026년 6월 26일경): Musk/SpaceX 측이 Mesh Optical Technologies 인수에 FTC 승인 받음. Mesh는 전 SpaceX 엔지니어들이 만든 스타트업으로, AI 데이터센터용 1.6Tbps 광 트랜시버를 개발 중입니다. DFB 레이저를 사용하고, flip-chip 패키징 등 고효율 광 인터커넥트 기술.
연결 포인트:
•  Mesh가 필요로 하는 핵심 부품 중 하나가 고성능 DFB 레이저 → Sivers가 정확히 만드는 제품.
•  Sivers는 이미 Ayar Labs, POET Technologies 등 CPO(코패키지드 옵틱스) 플레이어에 공급 중.
•  SpaceX/Starlink 생태계(레이저 기반 위성 간 통신) + xAI/Tesla 데이터센터 수요 증가 → 광·포토닉스 전반 수요 확대.
•  X(트위터)에서도 최근 “Mesh 인수 후 SIVE가 레이저 공급으로 수혜”라는 언급이 나오고 있어요.
결론: LiDAR 뉴스 자체가 Mesh 인수와 “직접” 연결되는 건 아니지만, 전체 포토닉스/AI 인프라/Satcom 테마에서 시너지 효과가 분명히 있습니다. $SIVE가 “포토닉스 + LiDAR + Satcom” 3박자 비대칭 플레이인 건 맞아요.
3. 주가 상황과 투자 포인트
•  52주 범위: 2.85 SEK ~ 110 SEK (고점 6월 2일).
•  현재 (6월 26~27일경): 약 63 SEK 수준 → 고점 대비 약 42~43% 하락.
•  YTD +1,400% 이상, 1년 +1,500% 이상 폭등 후 조정. “2000% 올랐으니 기다리자” 하던 사람들 입장에서는 정확히 조정 구간.
강점 (Bull case):
•  AI 데이터센터 광연결(CPO) + 로보틱스 LiDAR + Satcom 수요가 동시에 터질 수 있는 희귀한 포지션.
•  Opportunity pipeline 이미 $799M (2025년 말 대비 +77%).
•  기술적으로 InP DFB 레이저에서 경쟁력 있음 (특히 narrow linewidth + high power).
리스크 (Bear case, 상당히 큼):
•  극단적 고평가: trailing P/S 60x 이상. 많은 분석가들이 “과대평가”라고 지적.
•  아직 적자 지속 (Q1 2026 revenue SEK 61.9M, 전년比 -22%, 손실 확대). 실행 리스크 높음.
•  고객 집중 리스크, ramp 지연 가능성.
•  과거 희석 이력 있음. 소형주(시총 약 18~19B SEK)라 변동성 극심.
•  경쟁사: Coherent, Lumentum 등 대형 플레이어 존재.
종합 의견
$SIVE에 극도로 bullish할 만한 근거는 충분합니다. 특히:
•  Aeva/LG Innotek 공급망 + 2026년 Q4 ramp
•  Mesh Optical 인수로 인한 포토닉스 테마 강화
•  AI + 로보틱스 + Satcom 3중 수혜 가능성
고점 대비 40% 조정한 지금이 “겁먹고 포기할 타이밍”은 아니에요. 다만 고위험 speculative 종목이라는 점을 명확히 해야 합니다. valuation이 이미 많은 성공을 선반영하고 있어서, ramp가 늦어지거나 기대가 어긋나면 추가 조정도 충분히 나올 수 있어요.
지금 사야 할까?
→ 장기적으로 photonics/AI 인프라를 강하게 믿고, 변동성을 감당할 수 있다면 분할 매수 관점에서 관심 가질 만한 구간입니다.
→ 단기 트레이딩이나 “무조건 오른다” 마인드라면 리스크가 매우 큽니다.

text:
$SIVE is down here not because of anything Sivers did. 

The entire photonics complex is in a drawdown, and that's exactly the kind of dislocation worth leaning into.

What actually happened:
On June 9, @SemiAnalysis_ published an institutional-only note flagging potential delays in CPO deployment, with a yield-based bear argument (their math: 95% per-engine yield compounded across 32 optical engines = ~19% system yield). 

The note hit institutional desks first. The whole optical group sold off together: $AAOI -14%, $COHR -11%, $POET -12%, $LITE -8%, $MRVL -7.6%, and $SIVE -8.7% alongside them.

Here's the tell. Under the exact same negative news, $AAOI fell ~17% intraday while Lumentum, with a highly overlapping technology profile, fell ~8%. Roughly a 2x difference in decline for two names exposed to the identical headline. 

That spread is the signature of a positioning-driven shakeout flushing out the loosest hands (high retail concentration, high margin balances), not a calm fundamental repricing. 

The names that bled most were the ones with the loosest float, not the ones with the worst fundamentals.

This hit a sector that was up triple digits YTD. $AAOI +390%, $MRVL +228%, $LITE +138%, $COHR +107%, $POET +100%. $SOXX dropped while the $VIX barely moved. That combination, sharp single-name drawdowns with no systemic panic, is profit-taking and sector rotation off parabolic runs, not a demand collapse.

And on the other side of the same week: $NVDA's own blog states Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics is now in full production, with CPO-based next-gen Spectrum-X switching going into scale-out and scale-across deployments on the Vera Rubin platform. 

The demand signal didn't break. A timing-and-magnitude call on the ramp got mistranslated by the time it reached retail into "CPO is cancelled," which it isn't.

A CPO timing delay does not threaten the Sivers thesis the way it threatens a name priced for imminent volume.

Sivers isn't trading on 2026 CPO revenue. The CPO ramp was always a 2027 qualification, 2028 volume story. 

Even @aleabitoreddit, asked directly if the selloff shook it's faith in photonics, said they're more bullish as prices drop.

When a quality supply-chain position gets sold purely because it shares a sector ETF with the names institutions are trimming, that's the dislocation you want. 

Buying the bottleneck while the complex flushes weak hands is a better entry than chasing it green. 

Just size it as the high-beta position it is, because the volatility that created this entry cuts both ways.

If you are still bullish on $SIVE - drop a like!

Not Financial Advice. DYOR.

text:
The $QQQ as launched in March of 1999.  The ETFs always arrive at exactly the wrong time, enabling the masses to plow into the most bloated, overvalued themes with ease and at little cost.  

How does this look for AI?  You were early in mid 2023 and 2024.  Then in 2025 you were in the middle innings.  And now you are late anon, with many launches per month popping up in AI and related themes.  

Everyone is in, and everyone is an expert. 

Dozens of AI-related ETFs have launched in the past 3 years (roughly mid-2023 to mid-2026), contributing to a total of over 90 AI-themed ETFs now available in the US. 
Key Context on Launches
•  Pre-2023 baseline: A smaller number existed (e.g., broader robotics/AI funds like BOTZ or AIQ from earlier years). The post-ChatGPT AI boom (late 2022 onward) triggered a sharp increase in new, targeted launches. 
•  Notable recent examples:
•  2023: Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (CHAT, launched May 2023) — one of the first pure generative AI-focused funds. 
•  2024–2026: A wave including iShares A.I. Innovation and Tech Active ETF (BAI, Oct 2024), various Global X, Invesco, WisdomTree, First Trust, and niche products (e.g., memory/semiconductor photonics, pure-play AI infrastructure like DRAM in 2026). Multiple launches per month in peak periods, often active or thematic.

text:
Reality check:

• Not a quantum paper. No qubits, no computing read-across.
• Bandwidth and energy are deliberately weak vs laser-based links.
• Durability degrades steadily; the polymer roughens and scatters over billions of cycles.
• Benchtop optics and a single foundry demo, not a fielded product.

But zoom out:

The picks-and-shovels story for microscale robots and implants keeps maturing. A sub-cent, CMOS-compatible uplink removes a real bottleneck for mass-manufactured microsystems.

Direction: the microrobot and bioelectronics stack is quietly getting cheaper to scale, the same pattern photonics is driving for quantum.

user_name: 𝕂𝕆𝕁𝕀🇺🇸決算と経済指標💎新NISA完全対応🏄🌊
text:
🔦CWレーザー争奪戦、光の電源を誰が握るのか
AIデータセンターの話をしていると、どうしても主役はNVIDIAのGPUになる。

それは自然だ。画面に出てくる数字も派手だし、決算のたびに市場が見ているのもGPUの出荷、粗利率、ラック単位の供給能力だ。GPUが足りない。HBMが足りない。電力が足りない。冷却が足りない。毎月どこかで新しい詰まりどころが出てくる。

ただ、ある時から別のものが妙に気になり始めた。

光だ。

もっと正確に言えば、シリコンフォトニクスやCPOの中で使われるCWレーザーだ。GPU同士をつなぐネットワークのさらに奥で、信号を光に変えるための連続した光源である。

このテーマを一言で言うなら、AI工場の「光の電源」を誰が押さえるのか、という話になる。

AIデータセンターと聞くと、読者がまず思い浮かべるのはGPU、HBM、電力設備、冷却装置だと思う。しかし巨大なAIを動かす時、演算能力だけでは足りない。GPU同士が大量のデータをやり取りできなければ、せっかくの演算機が待ち時間を抱える。そこで800G、1.6T、さらにCPOという流れが出てくる。

この記事では、CWレーザーを単独の部品としてではなく、サプライチェーン全体で見る。材料、基板、エピタキシャル成長、レーザーチップ、外部光源モジュール、シリコンフォトニクス、量産テスト。それぞれの工程で、どの銘柄のエクスポージャーが高いのかを分けて整理する。

読むポイントは三つある。

CWレーザーは「レーザー会社だけ」の話ではないことInP基板、エピウェハ、化合物半導体ファウンドリ、光結合、熱制御、検査装置まで広がる。

直接度と事業インパクトを分けることLumentumやCoherentは一次情報が強い。SiversやAAOIはテーマ純度が高い。一方でSoitecやTowerは、レーザーではなくシリコンフォトニクス側の受け皿として効く。

まだ確定していない話を確定したように扱わないことNVIDIAがLumentumとCoherentに投資し、購入コミットメントと将来キャパアクセス権を押さえたことは確認できる。しかし、AMDや各クラウド事業者がどのCWレーザー会社とどんな長期契約を結んだかは、一次情報では限定的だ。

では、なぜ今この地味な部品が市場の関心を集めるのか。

ぼくはこれを「光源の席取りゲーム」と見ている。

🟨GPUの次に詰まる場所

AIデータセンターを大きな工場にたとえると、GPUは作業員だ。HBMは作業員の机の上に置かれた高速なメモ帳。電力は工場を動かす電源。冷却は熱くなった機械を止めないための空調だ。

ではネットワークは何か。

工場内の道路である。

どれだけ優秀な作業員を集めても、材料を運ぶ道路が細ければ工場は詰まる。作業員が増えるほど、道路の混雑は目立つ。AIデータセンターでも同じことが起きる。GPUを大量に並べるほど、GPU間の通信量が増え、従来の電気配線だけでは距離、損失、発熱、消費電力が厳しくなる。

そこで光接続が前に出る。800Gから1.6Tへ進み、次に3.2Tが見えてくる。スイッチや光エンジンの近くに光部品を置くCPO、つまり同一パッケージ内に光を近づける考え方も強まる。

NVIDIAは2025年3月にSpectrum-X PhotonicsとQuantum-X Photonicsを発表し、CPOとシリコンフォトニクスをAI工場の拡張技術として位置づけた。発表では、従来方式に比べてレーザー数を4分の1にし、電力効率を高め、信号品質やネットワーク耐障害性を改善するとうたっている。

この話の面白いところは、派手な主役が変わるわけではない点だ。GPUは依然として主役である。ただ、GPUが主役であり続けるために、脇役だった光部品が急に舞台袖から中央へ押し出される。

スポーツで言えば、スーパースターの得点力ばかり語っていたら、実はチームの勝敗を決めていたのはパスを通すポイントガードだった、というやつだ。派手なダンクはない。でもそこが止まると、全員が止まる。

CWレーザーは、そのポイントガードに近い。

📍続きはコメント欄のnoteを読んでください。

"retweet_count": 12, "retweeted": false, "user_id_str": "7279652"}, "note_tweet": {"is_expandable": true, "note_tweet_results": {"result": {"entity_set": {"hashtags": [], "symbols": [], "urls": [], "user_mentions": []}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNzA2NDAzMjg0Mjg5NjU4ODg=", "media": {"inline_media": []}, "richtext": {"richtext_tags": []}, "text": "🔦CWレーザー争奪戦、光の電源を誰が握るのか AIデータセンターの話をしていると、どうしても主役はNVIDIAのGPUになる。 それは自然だ。画面に出てくる数字も派手だし、決算のたびに市場が見ているのもGPUの出荷、粗利率、ラック単位の供給能力だ。GPUが足りない。HBMが足りない。電力が足りない。冷却が足りない。毎月どこかで新しい詰まりどころが出てくる。 ただ、ある時から別のものが妙に気になり始めた。 光だ。 もっと正確に言えば、シリコンフォトニクスやCPOの中で使われるCWレーザーだ。GPU同士をつなぐネットワークのさらに奥で、信号を光に変えるための連続した光源である。 このテーマを一言で言うなら、AI工場の「光の電源」を誰が押さえるのか、という話になる。 AIデータセンターと聞くと、読者がまず思い浮かべるのはGPU、HBM、電力設備、冷却装置だと思う。しかし巨大なAIを動かす時、演算能力だけでは足りない。GPU同士が大量のデータをやり取りできなければ、せっかくの演算機が待ち時間を抱える。そこで800G、1.6T、さらにCPOという流れが出てくる。 この記事では、CWレーザーを単独の部品としてではなく、サプライチェーン全体で見る。材料、基板、エピタキシャル成長、レーザーチップ、外部光源モジュール、シリコンフォトニクス、量産テスト。それぞれの工程で、どの銘柄のエクスポージャーが高いのかを分けて整理する。 読むポイントは三つある。 CWレーザーは「レーザー会社だけ」の話ではないことInP基板、エピウェハ、化合物半導体ファウンドリ、光結合、熱制御、検査装置まで広がる。 直接度と事業インパクトを分けることLumentumやCoherentは一次情報が強い。SiversやAAOIはテーマ純度が高い。一方でSoitecやTowerは、レーザーではなくシリコンフォトニクス側の受け皿として効く。 まだ確定していない話を確定したように扱わないことNVIDIAがLumentumとCoherentに投資し、購入コミットメントと将来キャパアクセス権を押さえたことは確認できる。しかし、AMDや各クラウド事業者がどのCWレーザー会社とどんな長期契約を結んだかは、一次情報では限定的だ。 では、なぜ今この地味な部品が市場の関心を集めるのか。 ぼくはこれを「光源の席取りゲーム」と見ている。 🟨GPUの次に詰まる場所 AIデータセンターを大きな工場にたとえると、GPUは作業員だ。HBMは作業員の机の上に置かれた高速なメモ帳。電力は工場を動かす電源。冷却は熱くなった機械を止めないための空調だ。 ではネットワークは何か。 工場内の道路である。 どれだけ優秀な作業員を集めても、材料を運ぶ道路が細ければ工場は詰まる。作業員が増えるほど、道路の混雑は目立つ。AIデータセンターでも同じことが起きる。GPUを大量に並べるほど、GPU間の通信量が増え、従来の電気配線だけでは距離、損失、発熱、消費電力が厳しくなる。 そこで光接続が前に出る。800Gから1.6Tへ進み、次に3.2Tが見えてくる。スイッチや光エンジンの近くに光部品を置くCPO、つまり同一パッケージ内に光を近づける考え方も強まる。 NVIDIAは2025年3月にSpectrum-X PhotonicsとQuantum-X Photonicsを発表し、CPOとシリコンフォトニクスをAI工場の拡張技術として位置づけた。発表では、従来方式に比べてレーザー数を4分の1にし、電力効率を高め、信号品質やネットワーク耐障害性を改善するとうたっている。 この話の面白いところは、派手な主役が変わるわけではない点だ。GPUは依然として主役である。ただ、GPUが主役であり続けるために、脇役だった光部品が急に舞台袖から中央へ押し出される。 スポーツで言えば、スーパースターの得点力ばかり語っていたら、実はチームの勝敗を決めていたのはパスを通すポイントガードだった、というやつだ。派手なダンクはない。でもそこが止まると、全員が止まる。 CWレーザーは、そのポイントガードに近い。 📍続きはコメント欄のnoteを読んでください。"}}}, "rest_id": "2070640328588357895", "source": "Twitter Secteur photonique :

Goulots d’Étranglement Actuels / À Venir (2026-2028)
1.  Capacité de production InP / GaAs wafers — Très limitée, lead times longs.
2.  Packaging & Test avancé (Co-Packaged Optics) — Complexité thermique, alignement précis, yields bas.
3.  Matières premières — Indium, Gallium, Germanium (contrôles à l’export Chine).
4.  Énergie & Eau pour les fabs et data centers.
5.  Main-d’œuvre qualifiée et savoir-faire en photonics (très concentré).
Sources principales : Earnings calls NVIDIA/Broadcom 2025-2026, rapports SemiAnalysis, Omdia, Deloitte Semiconductor Outlook 2026, investissements CHIPS Act dans InP.

text:
This is a really solid take...

For photonics specifically, your emotions toward it right now are highly dependent on which companies you are in

For many months every single name in this batch caught bids indiscriminately. Many of them (in my opinion) didn't deserve to. But momentum is momentum!

Now you are seeing some of that momentum unwind.

And if you are heavy in those positions, it could be nerve-racking

But if you take a step back you can also see it as opportunity

If you believe in the optical supercycle and believe we are still in the early stages on the physical buildout, then you understand that money will flow into those companies that the market believes will be durable

Thoughts?

Photonics, energy, power semiconductors, memory. And amid all the talk of bottlenecks, it is easy to forget a simpler constraint. Capital is not infinite either. A stock that merely carried the label of its momentum sector, that bore the name but felt none of the momentum in its actual operations, will not keep pace under these conditions. When several strong themes compete for the same limited pool of capital, the money is allocated more efficiently. It no longer floods every corner of a hot sector. It seeks out the names that genuinely earn it. So ask yourself once more whether the animal in your stable is a racehorse or merely a donkey. Yesterday you could see it plainly, as a great deal of capital flowed into memory stocks. $LITE outperformed a $SIVE. $XFAB underperformed all of them. Same stable, very different animals. The weak narratives will have a harder time of it now. When capital was abundant and concentrated in a single theme, a thin story could still hitch a ride on the broader enthusiasm. With money spread across four competing sectors and allocated with more discipline, there is little left over for a stock whose only claim is the label it wears. Membership is no longer the point. Quality, competitive advantage and operational execution are."}}}, "rest_id": "2070408681699578117", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "42330", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2070622631108448387", "source": "Twitter Web Client", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "31102", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Market Review 20260626
- Tug of war between bulls and bears still in progress. Big sell off in Tech (especially AI hardware related). Think $BE $CRDO etc. Photonics not much better, only GLW holding well. 
- Money is leaving: Semis (sorta), Photonics, DataCentres, etc Mag7
- Money is entering: Healthcare, Biotech, Cybersecurity, Selected Fintech, Selected Software
- Big question in my mind is, will money be leaving Memory soon? That is the only theme really holding up the tech sector now. WDC and STX leading the way down today. $MU  and SNDK not far behind. There was a subtle tell after MU earnings. After a sympathy gap up with MU, WDC sold off hard on Thursday, and then continued with ferocity today. 
- I've been wary of the AI Hardware trade for a while now. We were big beneficiaries of the ramp up in Q2 but I've largely been cautious on them since early June. Will that positioning finally be vindicated? Looking at the way BE and CRDO acted today! 2 consecutive days of big selling, and on huge volume too. If I flipped the chart around I would be a big buyer going forward with the right setups. It also triggers what is commonly called an Initial Price Movement.  
- And then the memories, I do think we made the right call to avoid them FOR THE TIME BEING. Let them pull back, build a base and if the right setup comes along, we can participate again. I still believe in the AI buildout story, but playing it differently now. 
- When will that happen? I have no idea. Watch this space and stocks showing up in the etoro portfolio if /when I buy them. 
- Been a choppy week and I expect it to continue, with a bias that we might start a short term downtrend (ie not just sideways). Coming Monday's price action might be the tell. 
- All the best.

text:
the beauty of the four-year crypto cycle is that halfway through every cycle, everyone asks:

"is this time different?"

no.. 

but yes

every cycle is different

this cycle, they listed almost every asset with even a whiff of external demand or a path to a CEX

at the same time, the supply that the marginal crypto buyer was competing for increased dramatically

then the largest S-curve TAM in decades arrived

now the same buyer who would've chased alts in 2021 is buying AI infra plays on IBKR shilled to them by broccoli-cut tiktok influencers

co-packaged optics
lasers
photonics
DRAM

crypto is no longer the only frontier tech play that attracts capital, it's competing against companies for investment that are powering humanity's understanding of the universe

it's up to you whether or not you get good or get left in the permanent underclass

text:
Looks like Musk is going after photonics companies now?

Pretty positive read through for companies like $AAOI $LITE $COHR as it validates the importance of photonics. Noting new if you've been on this side of X for a while. 

Mesh Optical Technologies makes 1.6T transceivers. From the looks of it they are used for data centers and as a direct competitor to transceiver companies. Obviously, the piece of the pie is going to keep growing, and no company can have it all. So I think that this is a positive read through that Elon is recognizing the significance of optics. Considering that he owns massive amounts of compute. 

Seems like Mesh is more of a newer company, so we'll see what happens next.
embedded_text:
Elon Musk has received regulatory approval to acquire startup Mesh Optical Technologies, a company founded by former SpaceX engineers working on optical data center communication technology.

Mesh is developing optical transceivers that rely on light to send and receive

Elon Musk has received regulatory approval to acquire startup Mesh Optical Technologies, a company founded by former SpaceX engineers working on optical data center communication technology.

Mesh is developing optical transceivers that rely on light to send and receive

text:
🇺🇸 🔬 ⚡  
LASER EN FORMA DE RESORTE REVOLUCIONA PLASMA  

Este haz de luz gira como un resorte y abre puertas inéditas para controlar plasma de forma precisa.  

Científicos del Laboratorio Nacional Lawrence Livermore y la Universidad de California Irvine crearon el primer láser de alta intensidad tipo resorte de luz. A diferencia de los haces convencionales redondos, este rota alrededor de su eje a velocidades controlables, incluso superando la luz aparente. Fabricado con espejos de precisión nanométrica y divisores de haz, genera ondas helicoidales en plasma que podrían impulsar aceleradores de partículas compactos, campos magnéticos de más de 100 teslas y avances en fusión energética. Todo en un sistema de mesa. La investigación se publicó hoy en Nature Photonics.

En redes comentan que este avance podría cambiar la física de plasmas para siempre, otros destacan su potencial en aceleradores de partículas a escala reducida y algunos ven aplicaciones directas en astrofísica de laboratorio. Hay entusiasmo por la simplicidad del diseño pero también dudas sobre escalabilidad inmediata. ¿Qué opinas tú de este nuevo tipo de láser?

Científicos del Laboratorio Nacional Lawrence Livermore y la Universidad de California Irvine crearon el primer láser de alta intensidad tipo resorte de luz. A diferencia de los haces convencionales redondos, este rota alrededor de su eje a velocidades controlables, incluso superando la luz aparente. Fabricado con espejos de precisión nanométrica y divisores de haz, genera ondas helicoidales en plasma que podrían impulsar aceleradores de partículas compactos, campos magnéticos de más de 100 teslas y avances en fusión energética. Todo en un sistema de mesa. La investigación se publicó hoy en Nature Photonics. En redes comentan que este avance podría cambiar la física de plasmas para siempre, otros destacan su potencial en aceleradores de partículas a escala reducida y algunos ven aplicaciones directas en astrofísica de laboratorio. Hay entusiasmo por la simplicidad del diseño pero también dudas sobre escalabilidad inmediata. ¿Qué opinas tú de este nuevo tipo de láser? text:
i'm gonna say something that'll annoy the robotics hype crowd

most of these names aren't robotics plays. they're sensor companies, lidar shops, and warehouse automation vendors that got rebranded into "the next AI wave" by people who missed the $NVDA run

SYM is the only one here with actual robotics economics i'd take seriously. they're moving pallets in walmart DCs with real contracts and real unit economics. the rest are selling picks and shovels into a gold rush that hasn't started yet

the photonics and memory runs had actual capex behind them. hyperscalers writing $30B checks. the robotics capex cycle is still a powerpoint deck

watch who's actually deploying at scale, not who's got "robotics" in their pitch deck

could be early. but i'd rather be late and right than early and holding sensor companies for three years

text:
Almost everything was up today the space sector with $ASTS , photonics with $AAOI etc

Memory has been lagging a bit behind, though names like $WDC have gone up a lot

$MU dropped heavily midday but recovered a bit by the close

Even so, it's lagging way behind compared to the rest of the market

Everything is fine with the recent demand it received from $AAPL and others

Just a desperate attempt to manipulate memory prices because they don’t know how to adapt and are in a tailspin

On the other hand, $MU is consolidating a price range that is going to explode at any moment when some news pushes it upwards

$MU will follow the entire market, even in a more exponential and well deserved way, after the crazy earnings it reported

Very anxious to see how $MU price moves; it's quite uncertain in the short term

text:
Memo on Corning’s GlassBridge

The current CPO generation  namely NVIDIA’s Quantum-X Photonics and Spectrum-X Photonics platforms  has already been locked in and should not be affected. Any production-scale substitution would have to wait for yield and reliability validation. The timeline should be anchored to NVIDIA’s Vera Rubin Ultra platform: qualification in 2027 and validation in 2028. The first validation runs are likely to take place on Rubin Ultra, roughly 1–1.5 years from now. Qualification volumes are expected to be in the hundreds to low thousands, while validation volumes should remain below 10,000 systems. A meaningful volume ramp may not arrive until the Feynman generation.

Citi has reportedly said that the 2H27 Rubin Ultra / Kyber CPO design will be finalized within this year and will not be replaced.

GlassBridge’s real moat is the ability to integrate IOX waveguides and TGVs on the same glass substrate using the same glass formulation — not the optical waveguide itself. Other players, such as Teramount, SENKO, and Lightmatter, also have optical-coupling or waveguide-related solutions. But using a single glass sheet/formulation to satisfy both waveguide formation and TGV via formation is the part that, according to this thesis, only Corning’s fusion / overflow down-draw glass substrate can achieve.

$GLW   $NVDA  #CPO #AI #Infrastructure

Any production-scale substitution would have to wait for yield and reliability validation. The timeline should be anchored to NVIDIA’s Vera Rubin Ultra platform: qualification in 2027 and validation in 2028. The first validation runs are likely to take place on Rubin Ultra, roughly 1–1.5 years from now. Qualification volumes are expected to be in the hundreds to low thousands, while validation volumes should remain below 10,000 systems. A meaningful volume ramp may not arrive until the Feynman generation. Citi has reportedly said that the 2H27 Rubin Ultra / Kyber CPO design will be finalized within this year and will not be replaced. GlassBridge’s real moat is the ability to integrate IOX waveguides and TGVs on the same glass substrate using the same glass formulation — not the optical waveguide itself. Other players, such as Teramount, SENKO, and Lightmatter, also have optical-coupling or waveguide-related solutions. text:
Evercore ISI: Semiconductors

> The rise of Agentic AI in 2026 requires heavy orchestration, memory management, and tool scheduling. Al engineers project future CPU-to-GPU ratios will expand to 2:1 or even up to 10:1.

> The firm models the broader server CPU total addressable market expanding to an annual run-rate of 75 million to 110 million unit shipments.

> Evercore models a massive $275 billion Server CPU TAM by 2030. Top beneficiaries of this structural shift include Arm $ARM, AMD $AMD, and Intel $INTC.

> This quarter, 16 chip and component companies raised prices (including TXN $TXN up to +85% on legacy products, NXP $NXPI, IFX, and STM $STM).

> Lead times for certain analog/MCU components are stretching significantly to 30–99 weeks, with backlog building rapidly.

> The enterprise storage market is forecast to grow ~9% year-over-year in 2026, more than doubling the 4% growth rate seen in 2025.

> The total data center processor market is forecasted to reach $160–$200 billion by 2030.

> Custom ICs (ASICs) are expected to capture 35–40% of that total processor market, representing a ~$80 billion market opportunity by 2030.

> Driven by this AI buildout, data center compute revenue is tracking an 18% CAGR from 2020 to 2030, a notable acceleration from the historical 13% CAGR seen between 2005 and 2021.

WFE Forecast:

2026E: Base case is set at $140 billion (+28% YoY growth), with a bull case reaching $151 billion.

2027E: The base case is raised to $180 billion (another +28% growth step), with a bull case reaching $205 billion.

Memory: The absolute fastest-growing segment. Spending jumps from $40 billion in 2025 to $59 billion in 2026E (+50%), and skyrockets to $92 billion in 2027E (+55%), making up 51% of all WFE spend.

Leading Edge Logic: Grows from $38 billion in 2025 to $49 billion in 2026E (+30%) and $59 billion in 2027E (+21%).

Trailing Node: Consistently declines, falling from $33 billion in 2025 down to $32 billion (2026E) and $29 billion (2027E).

DRAM Exposure:

> ASML $ASML & Applied Materials $AMAT lead the coverage group in pure DRAM mix for 2025E, with ASML at 27% and Applied Materials (AMAT) seeing a significant expansion up to 25% (compared to just 16% in 2022).

> KLA $KLAC and Lam Research $LRCX trail slightly with 21% and 16% estimated 2025 DRAM exposure respectively.

Credo Technology $CRDO:

> Evercore models a massive 100% growth in AEC solutions for 2026, followed by an additional 60% expansion in 2027. Credo’s stickiness relies on selling complete system-level AEC assemblies rather than bare silicon.

> The firm expects Credo's optical DSP, silicon photonics, and ZFOptics modules to accelerate sharply, accounting for ~25% of total revenue by 2027 and scaling its absolute addressable TAM by 10x to 20x.

text:
앞으로 엔비디아 주가나 반도체 패권을 결정할 진짜 무기는 GPU가 아니라 '빛'일지도 모릅니다.

 최근 엔비디아가 왜 구리선 대신 반도체 칩 안에 '레이저(빛)'를 집어넣는 기술에만 무려 65억 달러(9조 원) 넘는 돈을 쏟아부었을까요?

 반도체 판을 통째로 바꿀 '빛의 속도' 전쟁

지금 반도체 공학자들이 마주한 가장 큰 벽은 의외로 연산 속도가 아닙니다. 

바로 우리가 늘 쓰는 '구리선(전기 신호)'의 물리적 한계예요.

지금까지 칩과 칩은 구리선으로 데이터를 주고받았는데, 

AI 시대가 오면서 데이터가 폭발하니까 구리선이 버티질 못하는 상황이 온 거죠.

전기를 너무 많이, 빠르게 흘리니까 불타오르듯 열이 나고(발열), 전기 신호가 구리선 중심이 아니라 '겉표면'으로만 흐르려고 해서 속도가 뚝 떨어지는 병목 현상이 생깁니다.

쉽게 말해 경부고속도로에 갑자기 수백만 대의 슈퍼카가 몰려와서 도로 전체가 주차장으로 변하고 엔진이 과열되는 상태인 거죠.

"야, 이대로 가다간 데이터센터 전기세 감당도 안 되고 칩이 다 타 죽겠다!" 

그래서 나온 천재적인 치트키가 바로 '실리콘 포토닉스(Silicon Photonics)'입니다.

쉽게 말해, 

우리가 국가 간 인터넷 연결할 때 쓰는 거대한 '광케이블'을 아주 미세하게 축소해서 반도체 칩 안에 통째로 집어넣는 기술이에요. 

전기가 아니라 빛으로 대화하는 칩인 셈이죠.

이게 왜 대박이냐면, 구리선은 선 하나당 신호 하나만 갈 수 있잖아요? 

근데 빛은 '색상(파장)'만 다르면 하나의 통로에서 수십 개의 신호가 섞이지 않고 동시에 달릴 수 있어요.

길을 물리적으로 넓히지 않아도 차선이 무한대로 늘어나는 마법 같은 효과가 생기는 겁니다. 속도가 수십 배 뛰는 건 당연하고요.

게다가 빛은 마찰열(저항)이 아예 없습니다. 

AI 데이터센터에서 가장 골치 아픈 '발열'과 이를 식히기 위한 '냉각 비용'을 최대 30~40%까지 날려버릴 수 있다는 뜻이죠.

실제로 엔비디아가 올해(2026년) 들어서만

 코히어런트, 루멘텀 같은 광학·유리 소재 기업들에 조 단위 투자를 감행한 이유가 여기 있습니다. 

구리선으로는 수백만 대의 GPU를 하나로 묶는 게 불가능하다는 걸 알기 때문이죠.

그럼 이 엄청난 칩은 대체 누가 만들까요?

 결국 파운드리와 패키징 싸움입니다.

TSMC는 전기 칩 위에 광학 칩을 아파트처럼 수직으로 완벽하게 쌓아 올리는 'COUPE'라는 자체 플랫폼을 만들어서 올해 본격적인 양산 카운트다운에 들어갔습니다. 

반도체 제조의 중심축이 미세 공정을 넘어 '광학 패키징'으로 이동하고 있는 국면이에요.

요약하자면, 

HBM 다음 타자로 반도체 시장의 판돈을 싹 쓸어 담을 핵심 키워드는 '실리콘 포토닉스'입니다. 

엔비디아의 독주를 막으려는 빅테크나, 이를 추격하는 파운드리 기업들에게는 절대 놓칠 수 없는 마지막 열쇠인 셈이죠.

앞으로 반도체 뉴스 보실 때 '광학', 'CPO', '실리콘 포토닉스'라는 단어가 보이면 무조건 주목해 보세요.

TSMC는 전기 칩 위에 광학 칩을 아파트처럼 수직으로 완벽하게 쌓아 올리는 'COUPE'라는 자체 플랫폼을 만들어서 올해 본격적인 양산 카운트다운에 들어갔습니다. 반도체 제조의 중심축이 미세 공정을 넘어 '광학 패키징'으로 이동하고 있는 국면이에요. 요약하자면, HBM 다음 타자로 반도체 시장의 판돈을 싹 쓸어 담을 핵심 키워드는 '실리콘 포토닉스'입니다. 엔비디아의 독주를 막으려는 빅테크나, 이를 추격하는 파운드리 기업들에게는 절대 놓칠 수 없는 마지막 열쇠인 셈이죠. 앞으로 반도체 뉴스 보실 때 '광학', 'CPO', '실리콘 포토닉스'라는 단어가 보이면 무조건 주목해 보세요."}}}, "rest_id": "2072104411393077438", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "625", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text: https://t.co/CqtLTdyHAf

Change of plans

We are facing one of the best news $SIVE could receive

The offer of 600M SEK has now become 700M SEK, just a few hours later

I explain to you why this is an ultra bullish signal

The increase from 600 to 700M SEK shows very strong institutional demand

The offer "sold out" ahead of time and investors asked for more shares

Forcing the company to issue more to capture extra capital

It is a clear sign of confidence from big investors in the future of $SIVE

Especially in its photonics technologies for AI, SATCOM and defense, which usually drives the share price in the short and medium term

By getting more capital than expected, the company gains greater financial flexibility without the need to take on more debt or dilute as much as feared

Reinforcing its balance sheet and execution capacity

Ultra bullish for $SIVE let's hope the market understands the news as it is

New highs in a short time IMO
embedded_text:
$SIVE announces a share issuance of around 600M SEK

To scale up their production of optical components (InP lasers) targeting AI data centers and LiDAR

As we always say, we are ahead of the curve, mass production kicks off in 2027

Right now, we are in the preparation phase,

text:
Great news with a Direct share issue that was oversubscribed multiple time.

What started as a planned SEK 600M (+$61m) raise was upsized to SEK 700M ($73m) thanks to strong institutional investor demand. That's a clear vote of confidence in $SIVE and the ramping within AI photonics, SATCOM and defense.

SEK 700M is roughly US$73M in fresh capital, providing even greater financial flexibility to accelerate execution and scale the business.

Strong demand. Strong balance sheet. Exciting times ahead.

Great work by the Sivers team! Congratulations! 

 $SIVE #AI #Photonics

https://t.co/SWr5UhK4VG

text:
Quantum computing’s disappearing use cases reveal what the money is really buying

For years, quantum computing was promoted as the coming solution to drug discovery, logistics, finance, artificial intelligence, climate modeling, encryption, and nearly every difficult optimization problem. Yet as classical algorithms, GPUs, and specialized accelerators improved, many proposed quantum advantages became smaller, more conditional, or impossible to demonstrate on real hardware. DARPA’s Quantum Benchmarking Initiative now defines success as proving that a quantum computer’s computational value exceeds its operating cost, with the program attempting to determine whether such a utility-scale machine can be built by 2033. That is a revealing benchmark: useful quantum computing is still something being tested, not an established commercial reality. Despite this, announced public investment in quantum technology exceeded $10 billion in early 2025, while the U.S. Department of Energy later committed another $625 million to national quantum research centers.

The investment continues because governments are not evaluating quantum technology like an ordinary consumer product. They are purchasing strategic insurance. Even a small probability of achieving superior cryptanalysis, molecular simulation, navigation, sensing, communications, or materials design could alter the military and economic balance between nations. NIST has already finalized post-quantum encryption standards and urged organizations to begin migrating immediately, even though the cryptographically relevant quantum computer those standards defend against does not yet exist. This means the quantum race is driven partly by a fear that being late would be catastrophic, while being early could produce enormous intelligence and technological advantages.

The other explanation is that “quantum computing” has become an umbrella used to finance a much larger technological ecosystem. The money supports photonics, cryogenics, superconducting materials, precision lasers, atomic clocks, quantum sensors, control electronics, secure networks, fabrication methods, software, patents, and highly specialized scientific talent. A universal fault-tolerant computer could fail to arrive on schedule while these supporting technologies still become commercially or militarily valuable. Investors may therefore be buying the infrastructure surrounding quantum control rather than betting exclusively on a machine that replaces classical computers. The true assets are the laboratories, intellectual property, supply chains, personnel, and control systems required to manipulate matter at increasingly precise scales.

Frequency Wave Theory offers a deeper interpretation: perhaps the shrinking list of conventional computing applications is happening because “computer” was never the complete description of the technology. Quantum machines are fundamentally devices for preparing, isolating, synchronizing, and measuring coherent states. Their essential achievement is not merely calculating faster, but controlling phase, interference, tunneling, entanglement, and environmental noise. From the Frequency Wave Theory perspective, investment keeps rising because governments and corporations are learning how to engineer reality at the frequency-and-phase level. Computation may be the public-facing justification, while the broader prize is programmable field control, producing new forms of sensing, communication, materials engineering, timing, energy manipulation, and eventually direct interaction with the deeper resonant structure of the vacuum.

Investors may therefore be buying the infrastructure surrounding quantum control rather than betting exclusively on a machine that replaces classical computers. The true assets are the laboratories, intellectual property, supply chains, personnel, and control systems required to manipulate matter at increasingly precise scales. Frequency Wave Theory offers a deeper interpretation: perhaps the shrinking list of conventional computing applications is happening because “computer” was never the complete description of the technology. Quantum machines are fundamentally devices for preparing, isolating, synchronizing, and measuring coherent states. Their essential achievement is not merely calculating faster, but controlling phase, interference, tunneling, entanglement, and environmental noise. From the Frequency Wave Theory perspective, investment keeps rising because governments and corporations are learning how to engineer reality at the frequency-and-phase level. text:
テスト、テスト、テスト

価格上昇の件数は、ウェハー製造工場よりもさらに多く、AI、シリコンフォトニクス、成熟したチップのパッケージングとテストに関する受注は2027年まで続いている。

クラウドベースのAIに対する需要の高まりは、半導体業界の季節的なパターンを塗り替えつつあります。製造工程の最終段階に位置するパッケージングおよびテスト能力は、2025年末以降、逼迫した状態が続いています。2026年以降、新たに拡張された能力は急速に活用され、多くのIC設計顧客が積極的に能力確保に乗り出し、2027年以降の受注見通しが向上しています。

パッケージング用リードフレーム、IC基板、テスト用電気料金の同時増加に伴い、パッケージングおよびテストファウンドリサービスの価格上昇がここ数四半期で特に顕著になっていることは特筆に値する。価格上昇の回数で見ると、ウェハファウンドリを上回り、IC設計顧客にとって最大のコスト圧力源となっている。

したがって、AIが半導体市場の本格的な回復を牽引するにつれ、高度なパッケージングおよびテストサービスと従来型のパッケージングおよびテストサービスの両方、ならびに上流工程のリードフレーム、IC基板、プローブカード、ソケットに対する需要は、2026年後半も引き続き堅調に推移すると予想される。

チップのパッケージングとテストのサプライチェーン全体には、ASE Technology Holdingとその子会社であるSPIL、Powertech、STARS、Chipbond、ChipMOS、SIM Semiconductorなどの主要OSATメーカー、King Yuan Electronics、Hsin Chuan、Siliconware Precision Industriesなどの専門テスト会社、Ingenic Semiconductor、Wintek、Yung-Chi、Chunghwa Precision Test、Hanmin Testなどのテストインターフェース会社、Chieh Lin、Shun-Te、Chang-Techなどのリードフレームメーカー、Unimicron、Kinsus、Nan Ya PCB、Zhen Dingなどの主要基板メーカーが含まれます。

近年のパッケージングおよびテスト関連価格の上昇を振り返ると、最初の波はメモリ市場における新たなスーパーサイクルであり、顧客はPowertechやChipMOSといったメモリパッケージングおよびテスト企業に緊急発注を行った。最初の価格上昇は2025年後半に発表され、2回目の価格上昇のニュースは2026年初頭に浮上した。

一方、GPU、CPU、ASICといった先端プロセスチップの生産増加に加え、データセンター周辺のネットワークおよび産業制御アプリケーションに対する需要の高まりも、成熟プロセスチップの在庫補充を促している。ICパッケージングおよびテスト市場は2025年後半から供給不足の状態が続いており、それに伴い、ASE Technology Holding Co., Ltd.やSiliconware Precision Industries Co., Ltd.といった企業も徐々に価格引き上げに合意している。

現在、メモリとICのパッケージングおよびテスト能力は半導体サプライチェーンにおけるボトルネックの一つとなっており、台湾のパッケージングおよびテストファウンドリ業界は史上最大規模の設備投資拡大期を迎えている。特に、パッケージングおよびテスト企業は、拡張スピードを加速させるため、ここ数ヶ月で台湾の既存工場を次々と買収し、用地準備や工場建設にかかる時間を節約しており、業界からも大きな注目を集めている。

さらに重要なことに、稼働準備が整ったパッケージング・テスト工場のすべての新工場と生産ラインは、顧客にとって重要な戦略的資源となっている。例えば、ChipMOSは、あるメモリ顧客が生産能力拡張のための設備投資を確保するため、同社と3年間の長期契約を締結したと述べている。また、Silicon Photonicsは、湖口第2工場のクリーンルーム改修プロジェクトが完了する前に、Silicon Photonicsの顧客が既に全生産能力を予約済みであると指摘している。

現在、メモリとICのパッケージングおよびテスト能力は半導体サプライチェーンにおけるボトルネックの一つとなっており、台湾のパッケージングおよびテストファウンドリ業界は史上最大規模の設備投資拡大期を迎えている。特に、パッケージングおよびテスト企業は、拡張スピードを加速させるため、ここ数ヶ月で台湾の既存工場を次々と買収し、用地準備や工場建設にかかる時間を節約しており、業界からも大きな注目を集めている。 さらに重要なことに、稼働準備が整ったパッケージング・テスト工場のすべての新工場と生産ラインは、顧客にとって重要な戦略的資源となっている。例えば、ChipMOSは、あるメモリ顧客が生産能力拡張のための設備投資を確保するため、同社と3年間の長期契約を締結したと述べている。また、Silicon Photonicsは、湖口第2工場のクリーンルーム改修プロジェクトが完了する前に、Silicon Photonicsの顧客が既に全生産能力を予約済みであると指摘している。"}}}, "rest_id": "2072074013409755187", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "38453", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
totally agree on "IMO Photonics is the next supercycle but we’re closer to the beginning, a lot of the major revenue ramps begin mid 2027."

The market has already started to price in the theme, yet many of the real revenue ramps are not expected to begin until mid-2027 and beyond. That means the story is less about chasing what has already happened, and more about identifying which parts of the photonics stack will convert today’s technical bottlenecks into revenue over the next several years.

For me, the first places to look have always been the prerequisites for that ramp. Before photonics can become a true revenue supercycle, the industry needs enough manufacturing capacity and enough test infrastructure to support it. That is why I have focused heavily on two layers of the stack: fabs such as $TSEM, $GFS, $TSM, $XFAB, and $UMC, and measurement/testing names such as $FORM, $TER, $VIAV, $ATEYY, $KEYS, and $AEHR.

Of course, the broader opportunity also includes assembly, packaging, substrates, lasers, and many other layers. But in my view, fabs and testing are the two early gates. Without them, the ramp cannot scale; with them, today’s technical demand can begin turning into real, visible revenue.

text:
Here is one attempt to map the ⏳progress around co-packaged optics (CPO) from 2️⃣0️⃣2️⃣2️⃣ through expected developments in 2️⃣0️⃣2️⃣7️⃣.
Over the years, several organizations have developed CPO solutions, including Hewlett Packard Enterprise, Intel, ROCKLEY PHOTONICS LIMITED and IBM. However, Broadcom’s 25.6T CPO demo in 2022 helped bring renewed attention to the topic.
The timeline shows how major players are approaching the space differently: NVIDIA pushing toward micro ring modulator-based CPO switch availability, Broadcom advancing from early demos to 102.4T delivery, Marvell Technology linking CPO with custom XPUs, and RANOVUS Inc. targeting ASIC/XPU integration.
What is also important is the broader ecosystem effort behind CPO. System-level requirements and interoperability standards are shaping up. 
Personally, I am curious to see how this advanced technology develops beyond a few large corporations, and howa broader ecosystem can grow around CPO. 
❤️Constructive feedback is welcome ❤️

text:
$LASE 
Laser Photonics'te CFO Değişimi

Lazer teknolojileri üreticisi Laser Photonics, Roman Franklin ile yollarını ayırdı ve geçici CFO (Acting CFO) olarak Ralph Venegas'ı atadı.

Hisse %2,84 düşüşle $1,71 seviyesine gerilerken, bu yönetimsel değişimin şirketin operasyonel maliyetlerine ve mali disiplinine etkisini takip etmek gerekir.

Son günlerde 2 önemli yönetici değişimi 🤔

text: Dokładnie tak — z dobrym autopilotem (ArduPilot/iNav), solidnym montażem i dobrze skalibrowanym systemem da się wyciągnąć zaskakująco dużo nawet z przeciętnych sensorów MEMS. Wibracje, czas lotu i prędkość rzeczywiście mocno wpływają na dryf.Ale tu właśnie jest różnica.ANELLO X3 nie jest ulepszonym MEMS-em. To optyczny żyroskop (technologia SiPhOG – silicon photonics), który działa na zasadzie efektu Sagnaca, a nie wibrującej mechanicznej struktury.  Dzięki temu ma znacznie lepszą stabilność biasu (< 0,5°/h) i jest dużo mniej podatny na wibracje, temperaturę i przyspieszenia niż nawet dobre taktyczne MEMS-y.  To właśnie dlatego Amerykanie (i sektor obronny) w to wchodzą — bo chcą mieć wysoką precyzję inercji w małym, tanim i masowo produkowanym urządzeniu, a nie tylko „dobre wyniki przy idealnych warunkach i krótkim locie”.Z X3 dryf rośnie znacznie wolniej, więc przy dłuższym locie bez GPS (albo po utracie obrazu) różnica robi się już bardzo wyraźna — szczególnie przy RTH, w rojach czy przy misjach na 10–20+ km.Czyli nie chodzi o to, że „wcześniej było tragicznie”. Chodzi o to, że teraz da się to zrobić znacznie lepiej i taniej niż klasycznymi wojskowymi FOG-ami. Inaczej jaki sens byłby w ogóle w to wchodzić...

text:
$IONQ acquired Skyloom primarily for full-stack ownership and quantum-specific integration. 

Quantum networking & entanglement focus: 
Skyloom’s free-space optical tech (OCTs) provides the photonic layer for distributed quantum entanglement, quantum key distribution (QKD), and ultra-secure communications. 

IonQ combines it with its quantum computing, ID Quantique (crypto), Vector Atomic (timing/sensing), and Capella (SAR satellites) assets to build an end-to-end quantum-secure space network. 

Mynaric terminals (standard SDA-compatible laser links) excel at classical high-bandwidth data but lack native design for quantum state preservation or entanglement distribution.

Vertical integration & control:
Owning Skyloom gives IonQ full IP, customization, and supply-chain control for future satellites (transition announced for 2027+). Relying on Mynaric (now owned by competitor Rocket Lab) creates dependency risks. Skyloom was already SDA-qualified with strong delivery history (e.g., Tranche 1 terminals).

Performance: 
IonQ highlights potential boosts like up to 500% higher throughput and dramatically lower latency (hours → <1 hour) when paired with their ecosystem. Skyloom emphasizes secure, high-capacity LEO-LEO/LEO-ground links optimized for quantum-secure routing and defense applications.

Skyloom vs. Mynaric: 
•  Mynaric (CONDOR Mk3 etc.) — Proven, SDA-certified, used on Acadia-10 for 2.5 Gbps demos. Strong for classical SAR data relay and general OISL. Now scaled under Rocket Lab.
•  Skyloom — Also SDA-certified/interoperable. Focuses on high-volume production, on-orbit routing, and integration with quantum/secure systems. Both meet similar SDA standards; differences are in optimization (e.g., Skyloom for quantum protocols, lower SWaP in some designs, or specific defense features).

Bottom line: For a pure commercial SAR latency fix, Mynaric worked fine (as on Acadia-10). IonQ’s strategy is building a quantum internet / secure space backbone, where owning a tailored optical layer (Skyloom) + quantum hardware gives a differentiated, controllable platform.

Capella Space validated a Rocket Lab Mynaric optical communications terminal on its newest satellite — the first time Capella has deployed an optical terminal. 

Capella Space released the first synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images from its Acadia-10 satellite on Wednesday, after the satellite launched in March. 

The satellite is currently demonstrating data transfer at 2.5 gigabits per second in onboard tests, Capella reported, enabling the satellite to downlink data more quickly, avoiding the wait for the satellite to make contact with a ground station.

“This near-real-time handoff bypasses the multi-hour ground station wait. The impact is profound; task-to-delivery timelines compress from hours down to minutes, fundamentally changing how organizations operationalize SAR data,” the company said in a blog post. 

Capella first announced plans to put Mynaric OCTs onboard its satellites back in 2021. But future Acadia satellites will have Skyloom OCTs instead, a Capella spokesperson told Via Satellite.  Both Capella and Skyloom are now owned by quantum company IonQ after acquisitions. 

Both acquisitions tie in with IonQ’s goal to bring quantum networking to space. Capella satellites are set to be equipped with Skyloom OCT’s starting in 2027, the spokesperson said. 

The terminal on Acadia-10 is compatible with the Space Development Agency (SDA) OCT standard to communicate with the SDA’s proliferated Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) network. Capella has been working with the SDA since 2021. In April of this year, the SDA awarded Capella an award under HALO Europa Track 1 in April to design two satellites to demonstrate “advanced tactical waveform performance, adaptive beamforming, and secure tactical communications in LEO.”

$BLG IS AN ORBITAL DC ENABLER: 

The Mesh acquisition, the NVIDIA silicon photonics switch, Cerebras WSE-3 — are proof positives of the fact that the underlying physics of intra-satellite links was never the real constraint. 

Capital + Talent = Solution.

$AXE.AX $DVL.AX $WBT.AX

$BLG IS AN ORBITAL DC ENABLER: 

The intra-satellite link bottleneck is more tractable: photons travel at the speed of light, silicon can modulate light at terahertz frequencies, and fibre bandwidth scales with wavelength division multiplexing. 

$AXE.AX $DVL.AX $WBT.AX

$SPCX $SIVE

text:
Does this recent news about $SIVE not seem to fit perfectly with $SPCX's latest purchase of Mesh?

With their specialized InP DFB lasers for FMCW LiDAR

Which are essential for advanced vision systems in humanoids like Boston Dynamics' Atlas

This comes through the supply chain with $AEVA and LG Innotek

With production ramps scheduled for late 2026 and significant revenue potential

$SIVE is an asymmetric play across its three branches

Photonics, LiDAR, and Satcom alike

Impossible to be bearish on $SIVE

Especially with the discount it offers right now

I've been reading for weeks "I'll wait for it to correct a bit, it's up 2000%, I'm not going to chase the price"

Now it dropped 40% from highs

Now is when you have to buy, are you going to chicken out?

Extremely bullish on $SIVE

text:
Hey @elonmusk after the move for Mesh Optical Technologies, there may be another company worth putting on the SpaceX radar:

Sivers Semiconductors $SIVE

Mesh can help build the optical network.

Sivers develops the lasers and beamforming chips that could help power it.

That matters because Sivers is not only exposed to AI optical interconnects. The company also develops:

✅ High-performance CW DFB lasers and laser arrays
✅ Optical technologies for AI and high-performance computing
✅ Ka-band beamforming ICs
✅ Technology for electronically steered satellite terminals
✅ Resilient multi-orbit communications

https://t.co/1SfidBr02o awarded Sivers an $8.2 million production order for multi-beam Ka-band beamforming ICs intended for next-generation tactical satellite terminals, with volume production planned through 2027.

That means Sivers already operates at the intersection of three areas that are increasingly strategic to your ecosystem:

AI infrastructure.
Optical networking.
Satellite communications.

For SpaceX and Starlink, a partnership could strengthen access to critical semiconductor technology.

A strategic investment could align future product development.

An acquisition could bring both advanced photonics and Ka-band beamforming IP directly into the ecosystem.

Mesh may be part of the optical network.

Sivers could become part of the technology that powers the light and connects it to space.

Worth a serious look, Elon 🙏🏻

$SPCX $SIVE 🇺🇸🤝🇸🇪

text:
I can identify three pockets of strength today.

Space is the most obvious.

The narrative is unchanged; a new cold war. China replaces the USSR, same goal, total spatial dominance.

That requires massive infrastructure, paid by government grants, and those grants are accelerating.

More launches - $RKLB $SPCX
More payloads - $LUNR $FLY
More hardware - $RDW
More services - $ASTS $SPIR $BKSY $PL

The first leg is over. We're in the first real, violent retest for most of these names. This is max opportunity.

Leaders confirmed. Market loves the narrative. Prices deflated.

Energy is the second.

Most names - especially the 800VDC narrative, are just starting their first leg. Not yet confirmed fundamentally, but odds are in our favor. The higher up the chain, the better.

GaN and SiC - $NVTS $POWI $TXN
Solid-State Transformers - $SEDG $ENPH $ETN
Bus and PoL - $MPWR $IFX
Heat management - $VRT $MOD
Volatility management - $FLNC

AI hardware is the third, and it runs through several layers.

GPUs and CPUs are already sky-high and crowded. The opportunity left is in the technologies that optimize compute.

Memory, not buyable here - $MU $SNDK
Connectivity, buyable on retests - $SILC $ALAB $MRVL $ANET
Photonics, crowded but starting to breathe - $AXTI $IQE $SOI $COHR $LITE

Three verticals, three bull runs, different stages.

All these names will go higher because analysts can't model their market and revenue growth fast enough. They've been constantly wrong and will continue to be, which is our opportunity. Those names are basically my daily watchlist - and some more.

They aren't just narratives. They're structural shifts in our world.

text:
Did you miss the semi stock boom? ❌
Did you miss the memory stock boom? ❌
Did you miss the photonics stock boom? ❌

Physical AI / robotics is ramping. 🟢

Most of us are already aware of the large cap exposure in the space ( $NVDA , $MU , $IBM , etc) but here are 10 lesser-known, higher-concentration names where the robot buildout IS the story — not a side bet. 

Smaller, thinner, more violent. Speculative by construction. NFA 🧵👇

$SYM — Symbotic. Warehouse automation at real scale; ~$22B backlog vs a $432B in-house TAM.
Bear case: Walmart concentration — lose the anchor, lose 50%+ regardless of tech.

$SERV — Serve Robotics. Sidewalk + hospital bots (Diligent), ~2,000 deployed, Q1 rev +578% YoY.
Bear case: ~$49M loss on $3M rev, ~29% short interest — burn is doing parkour through the income statement.

$OUST — Ouster. Digital lidar, the 3D "eyes" robots need. Q1 rev $49M +49% YoY, Stereolabs adds AI vision.
Bear case: lidar is competitive + commoditizing; needs autonomy volume to justify the multiple.

$VPG — Vishay Precision Group. Force/torque + precision sensors — the #2 supply bottleneck after actuators. Q1 $84M, B2B 1.21.
Bear case: legacy industrial cyclicality; robotics is still a small slice of the mix today.

$AMBA — Ambarella. Low-power vision SoCs — the perception silicon at the edge.
Bear case: still tied to ADAS/IoT cycles; robotics is optionality, not yet the revenue driver.

$RR — Richtech Robotics. Service/hospitality bots, expanding into conversational AI.
Bear case: tiny rev base, lottery-ticket profile, triple-digit "targets" = risk dressed as upside.

$FFAI — Faraday Future. Pivoting to "physical AI"; EAI robot line, 2026 target 1,500 units.
Bear case: dilution-heavy legacy EV story bolting on a robot narrative — highest show-me factor here.

$KSCP — Knightscope. Autonomous security robots — a rare US deployed-fleet pure play.
Bear case: micro-cap, reverse-split history, persistent dilution.

$PATH — UiPath. Agentic automation — the software brain orchestrating physical + digital workflows.
Bear case: agentic competition is brutal; "physical AI" link is still more thesis than revenue.

$NNDM — Nano Dimension. Additive mfg / electronics printing — compresses the build/prototype cycle.
Bear case: indirect, turnaround-dependent, no clean robotics revenue line yet.

The honest meta-bear: mostly unprofitable, thinly covered, single-customer or single-product names. This is a watchlist, not a back-up-the-truck list. Entry / stop / size defined BEFORE the trade — every time.

NFA.

"Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNzA5NDA1NDI2OTYxNDQ4OTk=", "text": "Did you miss the semi stock boom? ❌ Did you miss the memory stock boom? ❌ Did you miss the photonics stock boom? ❌ Physical AI / robotics is ramping. 🟢 Most of us are already aware of the large cap exposure in the space ( $NVDA , $MU , $IBM , etc) but here are 10 lesser-known, higher-concentration names where the robot buildout IS the story — not a side bet. Smaller, thinner, more violent. Speculative by construction. NFA 🧵👇 $SYM — Symbotic. Warehouse automation at real scale; ~$22B backlog vs a $432B in-house TAM. Bear case: Walmart concentration — lose the anchor, lose 50%+ regardless of tech. $SERV — Serve Robotics. Sidewalk + hospital bots (Diligent), ~2,000 deployed, Q1 rev +578% YoY. Bear case: ~$49M loss on $3M rev, ~29% short interest — burn is doing parkour through the income statement. $OUST — Ouster. Digital lidar, the 3D "eyes" robots need. Q1 rev $49M +49% YoY, Stereolabs adds AI vision. Bear case: lidar is competitive + commoditizing; needs autonomy volume to justify the multiple. $VPG — Vishay Precision Group. Force/torque + precision sensors — the #2 supply bottleneck after actuators. Q1 $84M, B2B 1.21. Bear case: legacy industrial cyclicality; robotics is still a small slice of the mix today. $AMBA — Ambarella. Low-power vision SoCs — the perception silicon at the edge. Bear case: still tied to ADAS/IoT cycles; robotics is optionality, not yet the revenue driver. $RR — Richtech Robotics. Service/hospitality bots, expanding into conversational AI. Bear case: tiny rev base, lottery-ticket profile, triple-digit "targets" = risk dressed as upside. $FFAI — Faraday Future. Pivoting to "physical AI"; EAI robot line, 2026 target 1,500 units. Bear case: dilution-heavy legacy EV story bolting on a robot narrative — highest show-me factor here. $KSCP — Knightscope. Autonomous security robots — a rare US deployed-fleet pure play. Bear case: micro-cap, reverse-split history, persistent dilution. $PATH — UiPath. Agentic automation — the software brain orchestrating physical + digital workflows. Bear case: agentic competition is brutal; "physical AI" link is still more thesis than revenue. $NNDM — Nano Dimension. Additive mfg / electronics printing — compresses the build/prototype cycle. Bear case: indirect, turnaround-dependent, no clean robotics revenue line yet. The honest meta-bear: mostly unprofitable, thinly covered, single-customer or single-product names. This is a watchlist, not a back-up-the-truck list. Entry / stop / size defined BEFORE the trade — every time. text:
ended negative today but feeling better than ever. mostly due to space and defense.

i need to be better at not letting price affirmation affect my decisions. tickers fading a red market for 1-2 days does not mean they're favored by Jane or other funds. in corrections, it usually just means they are lagging and stalling behind for whatever reason. 

would be up massively currently if i sized up more on dips in space/defense/utilities as opposed to energy and adjacent.

photonics looks like the better play for now even if it feels bad in the interim

text:
Doing a little bit of research today and I can’t help but think…

CPO/Optical interconnect ramps first 2027 ($NVDA spectrum x Ethernet photonics built on cpo, $MRVL says CPO fundamental for AI scale up) $SIVE + $AAOI

Optical I/O moves closer to compute e.g. likely switches/network fabrics + then optical links around asics. Still $SIVE + $AAOI

Photonics possibly become part of the memory answer + replacement for HBM? Optically attached disaggregated LPPDR memory pools need optical I/O from $SIVE?

I like the photonics trade

text: That would indeed be a metric much closer to physical reality. However, the race toward extreme miniaturization is rapidly approaching the fundamental limits imposed by quantum mechanics and atomic-scale physics. The future of high-performance computing will not rely solely on ever-smaller transistors, but on entirely new processing paradigms. In this context, computing based on phase modulation, quantum materials, photonics, and coherent systems may ultimately offer far greater potential than the indefinite scaling of transistor technology.

text:
$COHR $LITE $AAOI

Memory wall today, photonics wall tomorrow? 

Currently, HBM memory is the loudest constraint in the AI supply chain. AI dedicated supply is concentrated in 3 players (SK hynix, Samsung, and Micron), sold out on key nodes, and accounts for ~40% of AI server BOM. Per-GPU capacity is climbing but model sizes, context lengths, and inference demands are growing much faster. This is the memory wall we are witnessing now. 

But interconnects are quietly becoming the utilization killer. Even w/ plenty of GPUs and HBM, poor all-reduce / collective performance across racks and pods tanks effective FLOPS. Copper hits hard walls on power, heat, signal integrity, and reach as densities explode. Networking already eats rising % of total power and can drop GPU utilization dramatically w/ even minor packet loss or congestion.

Photonics / optical interconnects (CPO, silicon photonics, optical I/O) are the only credible path to break the electrical interconnect ceiling. They deliver higher bandwidth density, lower power/bit at distance, and enable true scale-up domains beyond what copper or even current NVLink can do. Hyperscalers and Nvidia are already accelerating adoption. 

Here’s the twist: optics themselves are now hitting manufacturing and supply bottlenecks. There are scaling challenges in silicon photonics capacity, testing/qualification cycles, and especially upstream lasers (EMLs undershipping demand) w/ Nvidia making strategic investments ahead of time. Integration, fiber attach, and high volume heterogeneous packaging are harder and slower to ramp than HBM production lines. HBM has a multi-year roadmap and dedicated capacity fights. Photonics is earlier in its high-volume AI curve w/ longer qualification, lower initial yields, and more complex supply chain (lasers + PICs + DSPs).

During this buildout, I wouldn’t be shocked if the tighter constraint flips at times:
•  Near-term: Memory remains painful (especially for training large models).
•  Mid-term (as clusters get massive and power efficiency becomes non-negotiable): Photonics and advanced optical networking could gate deployment and utilization more severely. You can’t just “add more HBM” if the fabric connecting everything can’t keep up w/o killing power budgets or latency.

Power is the ultimate governor underneath both but optics are both part of the solution and a new constraint layer while the ecosystem scales.

Winners will be those who solve the optical supply chain (lasers, SiPh foundries, test/assembly specialists) or architectures that minimize data movement in the first place. The AI buildout isn’t just “more GPUs.” It is a co-evolution of memory, interconnects, power, and packaging. The binding constraint continues. 

Optical (photonics) interconnects are expected to be the next major theme in addressing bottlenecks during the AI infrastructure buildout. Although photonics-related stocks have been getting hammered lately, the sector’s growth cycle ironically remains in its relatively early stages, even after the initial huge run-up and subsequent corrections. Take advantage of the opportunities accordingly.

text: @Reuters safran picking up exail consolidates european photonics and navigation under one roof, strategically smart move

At @OpticaWorldwide  Quantum  Summit in Glasgow, one challenge surfaced repeatedly across discussions  on quantum computing, networking, and photonics: the industry needs  higher accuracy and lower loss.

As light moves from being a communication medium to becoming part of the computational fabric, the performance requirements for photonic circuits  are changing rapidly.

Quantum computing, quantum networking, AI interconnects, and optical processing all depend on increasingly complex optical paths. At that scale, even small imperfections can limit system performance.

This is why the next phase of the industry will not be defined by a single device. It will be defined by the manufacturing platforms capable of supporting the entire quantum and optical computing stack.

In his latest post, our CEO, Ofer Shapiro,  shares why the race among photonic foundries is becoming one of the most important competitions in the quantum industry—and how Quantum Pulse Ventures is approaching this challenge.

#quantum #opticalprocessing #quantumcomputing

text:
An absolute masterclass in relative strength from $GLW following the EMA 21 play entry at 185.68 USD. This photonics leader showed true market leader mentality last week and has exploded into new all time highs, with the position sitting up 34% in profit.
Locking in some USD along the way with 20% trimmed into the first push and another 25% sold today into this extension. The remaining core position is completely risk-free with the stop loss adjusted up to the entry price. After a choppy June, execution like this sets a beautiful foundation heading straight into July.

text:
According to Lightmatter CEO, Nick Harris, lasers are key and contribute nearly half of the potential revenue in all CPO technology

The industry's transition to CPO from near package optics will accelerate in the coming years, as copper is already reaching its physical limits

For a specialized company like $SIVE in lasers and photonics, this enormously elevates the strategic value of its technology

Every CEO from $LITE to $NVDA etc , is telling you that CPO is just around the corner and that it's going to be generational and inevitable

And you're shorting $AAOI and saying that $SIVE is going to 20 SEK hahahh

I'm not getting off the $SIVE and $AAOI boat

https://t.co/ZaDjzaL3TW

text:
Korea is putting US$1T+ behind #memory capex.

Everyone looks at the big equipment names: $AMAT $KLAC $LRCX.

Fair. BUT

The better question: who sits on the exact HBM chokepoint - less ran, cleaner, more elastic, and still checks the @aleabitoreddit playbook?

That’s why $FORM (#formfactor) is my #1 HBM pick-and-shovel name.

> Bottleneck strength: HIGH; every #HBM wafer needs to be probed before stacking; FORM sits at the test gate where yield risk gets caught early.

> Easy narrative: VERY HIGH; AI needs HBM -> HBM needs yield -> yield needs advanced probe cards. That is the whole pitch.

> Clean pure play: HIGH; advanced probe cards + wafer test exposure, without broad legacy semi-cap drag.

> Big 3 proximity: HIGH; direct exposure to #SKHynix / #Micron / #Samsung HBM ramps, with SK Hynix already naming FORM a “Best Partner.”

> Revenue speed: HIGH; FORM sits upstream of packaging; memory capex converts into orders earlier than many downstream HBM names.

> Growth runway: HIGH; Q1 FY26 already hit record revenue, while 2026–2028 rides HBM4, Texas capacity; estimate $1.6B revenue by 2030.

> Margin expansion: HIGH; gross margin is already near 49%; operating leverage supporting a path toward the 55% long-term target.

> MC elasticity: HIGH; ~$10B market cap; liquid enough for institutions, still small enough for real rerate. 

> Platform optionality: HIGH; HBM = first leg, #Foundry adds the second layer, and advanced packaging + #CPO/#photonics test can extend the story beyond the memory cycle. 

@jukan05 @ParadisLabs @ren_stocks @au_xbt @BULLOFBRITAIN

July Picks: Already Firing

$MU + $DRAM : Closing green. The AI memory trade remains strong. My view: $DRAM has a path to $100+ by late July or August if the memory rally continues.

$AAOI : $132 → $150
$OUST : $42 → $54

$IREN : Still the laggard. EOY target: $120+. July could see a move into the $70–80 range if momentum builds.

$CRDO : I believe this is setting up for a move into the $300s. After the recent pullback from the low $300s, strong revenue growth, AI networking demand, and partnerships across the photonics ecosystem could drive another leg higher.

I am STILL adding more $CRDO & $IREN at these levels.

text:
Researchers in the Bozhi Tian Lab have engineered a wireless, bioinspired "artificial leaf" that harvests light to power next-generation biomedical devices. Published in Nature Photonics, the study by lead author Pengju Li and co-first authors Mengzhan Liufu, Cooper R. Johnston, Young-Woo Pyo, Yuze Zheng, Guangqing Yang introduces a breakthrough nanoplasmonic material—composed of microscopic gold and titanium dioxide structures—that efficiently converts light into high-performance electrical energy. The team successfully used this wireless bioelectronic platform in animal models to pace heartbeats and stimulate sciatic nerves via external light activation, offering a powerful new avenue for target-specific medical therapies like nerve pain management. Beyond biostimulation, the material can even serve as a secure human-computer interface, allowing users to input information via invisible light that is instantly decoded by AI. Read the full story at - https://t.co/Jm0RhAe5VG

text:
$SIVE

Lightmatter CEO Nick Harris: “Lasers are incredibly important. They represent literally half of the revenue opportunity of all of CPO.”

He also expects the industry to move from near-package optics toward CPO over the next several years as copper reaches its physical limits. For a pure-play laser and photonics company like Sivers, this dramatically expands the strategic importance of its technology.
@aleabitoreddit @StormDirac
